checks.a_analysis: The output document structure corresponds to the source PDF content. The metadata title 'Logo Imagery Screenshot Imagery Fca1' is an acceptable AI-generated placeholder for accessibility. No evidence of fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or sections was found during the audit.

All identifiable contact information in the output, specifically the URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/', was verified against the source PDF. No fabricated URLs, phone numbers, or email addresses were detected. While some URLs are omitted in certain sections, the ones present are correct.
The audit confirmed that all three pages and all eight primary instructional headings from the source PDF are present in the output JSON. No complete page or section has been omitted.
The source PDF contains no tables; therefore, this check is not applicable and results in a pass.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a recurring and significant omission of critical instructional text on pages 2 and 3. In the sections 'How to reset your password', 'How to change your password', 'How to update your profile', 'How to enroll Open Subscription Applications', and 'How to delete your account', the URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' is consistently missing from Step 1. Furthermore, specific button labels required for following the instructions (e.g., 'Submit', 'Reset', 'SAVE') are omitted from the text of the steps in these same sections. This omission renders several steps non-functional (e.g., 'Click on: ' instead of 'Click on: SAVE') and represents a substantial loss of instructional information across multiple sections, qualifying as a Major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2, 'How to change your password': '1. Go to https://myncid.nc.gov/' and '5. Click on: SAVE'. Page 2, 'How to update your profile': '1. Go to https://myncid.nc.gov/' and '4. ...click on: SAVE'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 2, reading_order 34: 'Go to ' and 'Click on: '. Section 2, reading_order 37: 'Go to ' and 'Edit the values that you want to update, click on: '.
checks.b_evidence_location: Instructions on Page 2 and Page 3 (reading orders 31, 34, 37, 55, 58).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Page 1 instructions are accurate. The systematic errors on pages 2 and 3 are identified as significant omissions under C5. No other minor text discrepancies affecting meaning were found.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains multiple sections with repeated steps (e.g., 'Go to https://myncid.nc.gov/' or 'Trouble Signing On?'). These repetitions are intentional and reflect the source document's design where each instructional guide is standalone. No unintentional duplication of text blocks or sections created by the conversion pipeline was observed.

The heading hierarchy is preserved correctly. The main title 'MyNCID User Guide' is assigned level 1, and all sub-headings for the various guides (e.g., 'How to register', 'How to unlock your account') are assigned level 2. There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.
The source document does not contain any tables. The multi-column layout consists of headings and lists, which have been correctly identified as such.

The textual content flows logically and follows the page-by-page, column-by-column sequence of the source. While images (buttons and screenshots) have been moved to the end of their respective page sections (e.g., reading_order 17-26 for page 1), the instructional sequence of the text remains intact. This reordering is considered a minor adjustment for accessibility and does not break the logical flow of the guide.

The document's numbered lists have been converted to unordered lists. Furthermore, the explicit numbering (e.g., '1.', '2.') present in the source text has been stripped during conversion. Some list items are also rendered incomplete because the text referring to a visual button was not captured in the text flow (e.g., the items ending in colons). This represents a loss of structural meaning for a step-by-step guide.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, 'How to register' section contains a numbered list from 1 to 6. Page 2, 'How to reset your password' step 8: '8. Click on: [Reset button image]'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in elements index 3 and 4 is 'unordered' and lacks numbering. Item 8 in element index 31 reads only 'Click on: ' with no following text.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains several instances of the URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/'. While the URL text is extracted correctly, it is not marked as a clickable hyperlink within the JSON structure, which is a structural omission for digital documents.
checks.b_evidence_source: URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' appearing throughout the document (e.g., Page 1, Step 1).
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Go to https://myncid.nc.gov/" (The text is present but lacks hyperlink metadata).

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly compromised. Key instructional elements, specifically the 'SAVE' and 'CONFIRM' buttons which belong to the 'How to register' section on Page 1 of the PDF, have been detached from their relevant text and rendered far down the page after the 'How to retrieve your username' section. This breaks the logical visual flow and relationship between the instructions and the visual aids intended to accompany them.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, 'How to register' section includes steps 4 and 5 with associated button images for 'SAVE' and 'CONFIRM' immediately following the text.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'SAVE' and 'CONFIRM' buttons are located in a block of images positioned after the 'How to retrieve your username' section, disconnected from the registration instructions.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML body, between 'How to retrieve your username' and 'How to reset your password' headings.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF does not contain traditional grid-based tables. It consists of instructional text and screenshot callouts. No table rendering issues are present as no tables exist.

The source PDF contains several meaningful screenshots that act as visual aids for the user guide. A critical image—the screenshot of the 'delete' confirmation modal from Step 4 of 'How to delete your account' (Page 3)—is entirely missing from the HTML output. Additionally, while some buttons are rendered as text or separate assets, the visual context provided by the screenshots in the PDF (like the yellow-bordered box for registration on Page 1) is lost or incomplete.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3, 'How to delete your account' section, step 4 shows a screenshot of the confirmation modal with a text input field containing 'delete' and buttons for 'CANCEL' and 'DELETE'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The confirmation modal screenshot is not rendered in the HTML output.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML 'How to delete your account' section.

checks.a_analysis: There is significant visual duplication in the rendered HTML that does not exist in the source. Specifically, the 'Forgot Username / Forgot Password / Unlock Account' screenshot block and the large MyNCID branding logo with a lock and black background are repeated multiple times within the body of the document, creating visual clutter and confusion.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF contains unique instances of screenshot blocks within their respective sections (e.g., 'Forgot Username...' appears once in the context of retrieving a username on Page 1 and once for resetting a password on Page 2).
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Forgot Username / Forgot Password / Unlock Account' block appears twice in the main body area in close proximity. The large MyNCID brand logo also appears twice in the body area.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML body, primarily in the middle section between headings.
